# Date Time Input

Edits a date and optional time as keyboard-friendly segments.

## Installation

Add Date Time Input from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/date-time-input
```

## Usage

Date Time Input is a StackTrace wrapper around Reka Date Field. It renders editable segments without a calendar popover. Bind `v-model` to an `@internationalized/date``DateValue`, choosing the value class according to the domain meaning.

### Date only

Use `CalendarDate` for a day that has no time or time zone, such as a birthday or due date. With a date-only value, day is the natural granularity.

**Date only example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="grid w-[280px] gap-2">
    <Label for="due-date">Due date</Label>
    <DateTimeInput
      id="due-date"
      v-model="value"
    />
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { DateTimeInput } from '@/Components/DateTimeInput'
import { Label } from '@/Components/Label'
import { CalendarDate } from '@internationalized/date'
import { ref } from 'vue'

const value = ref(new CalendarDate(2026, 7, 11))
</script>
```

### Date with time

Use `CalendarDateTime` for a wall-clock date and time whose zone is handled elsewhere. Set `granularity` to `hour`, `minute`, or `second` according to the precision users can meaningfully provide.

**Date with time example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="grid w-[280px] gap-2">
    <Label for="starts-at">Starts at</Label>
    <DateTimeInput
      id="starts-at"
      v-model="value"
      granularity="second"
      locale="en-GB"
    />
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { DateTimeInput } from '@/Components/DateTimeInput'
import { Label } from '@/Components/Label'
import { CalendarDateTime } from '@internationalized/date'
import { ref } from 'vue'

const value = ref(new CalendarDateTime(2026, 7, 11, 14, 30, 0))
</script>
```

## Zoned values and display options

Use a `ZonedDateTime` when the zone is part of the value. Reka can display its time-zone segment; set `hide-time-zone` only when the zone remains unambiguous elsewhere. The fallthrough API also supports `locale`, `hour-cycle`, `step`, and `step-snapping`. Locale changes the segment presentation, not the meaning of the stored value.

## Constraints and states

Pass `min-value`, `max-value`, or `is-date-unavailable` to constrain entry. Use `disabled` when the field cannot be interacted with and `readonly` when its value should remain focusable but cannot be edited. Keep every bound value and constraint in a compatible calendar and time representation.

## Keyboard and accessibility

Reka gives each segment spinbutton-like editing, segment navigation, and localized literals. Associate the root `id` with a visible label, and do not override arrow keys or character entry. Explain granularity in the label or description when seconds or a time zone are required. Use `aria-invalid` and an associated error message only after validation has run.

## Choosing a date control

> **Use Date Input for typing plus a calendar: [Date Input](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/date-input)**
>
> For date-only ISO string state that also needs visual calendar selection, use Date Input.

> **Use Date Picker for simple selection: [Date Picker](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/date-picker)**
>
> If users only need to choose a date from a popover, Date Picker avoids the additional segment-editing interaction.

Hosted documentation: [Date Time Input](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/date-time-input)
